tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: One-Legged Teenager Scores Basketball Dreams—Father's Wooden Crutch Becomes His Wings
stories.summary: Thirteen-year-old Apei from rural China played basketball with his father's handmade crutch before receiving a custom prosthetic to chase his dreams.

stories.headline: Chris Nikic Becomes First Ironman with Down Syndrome—and Inspires a Race Guide's Dreams for His Son
stories.summary: Chris Nikic became the first person with Down Syndrome to complete a full Ironman, guided by Dr. Tommy Martin, whose autistic son Oliver now has a new hero.

stories.headline: Against All Odds: Raul Jiménez Scores First World Cup Goal at 35, Tears for His Father
stories.summary: At 35, Raul Jiménez scored his first World Cup goal and wept for his late father, six years after doctors called his survival from a skull fracture a miracle.

stories.headline: Teenage Volunteers Answer Prayers With New Roofs and Hope for Eastern Shore Families
stories.summary: Teenagers from Richmond, led by 72-year-old retired priest Churchill Pinder, spent four days repairing homes for low-income families on the Eastern Shore.

stories.headline: Keene's Free Store: Strangers Gathering Weekly to Lift Up Their Unhoused Neighbors
stories.summary: Volunteers gather every week at Railroad Square to distribute donated food, toiletries, and supplies—no questions asked, just neighbors helping neighbors through hardship.

stories.headline: Tiny Ultrasound Sticker Mends Hearts Without Surgery—A Breakthrough for Millions
stories.summary: MIT researchers created a postage-stamp-sized ultrasound sticker that safely restores healthy heartbeats without invasive surgery, tested successfully on rats.

stories.headline: Fusion Breakthrough: Scientists Hold the Sun's Power Steady for 43 Seconds
stories.summary: Germany's Wendelstein 7-X reactor sustained fusion plasma for a record 43 seconds, stretching the window for steady-state clean energy by an order of magnitude.

stories.headline: Wind Turbines Now Grow Fertilizer—Minnesota Plants Seeds for a Greener Farm Future
stories.summary: A Minnesota plant now harnesses wind to transform air and water into ammonia, offering farmers affordable fertilizer and a path to sustainable agriculture.
